# Phase 10.0.4 Lineage Continuity Plan

Status: Planning-only. This plan does not create executable workflows, runtime authority, provider authority, infrastructure authority, deployment authority, autonomous governance, APIs, background jobs, automated succession authority, governance self-transfer systems, or execution authority.

## Objective

Define how civilization preserves constitutional legitimacy, governance identity, and institutional continuity across multiple generations of operators, maintainers, institutions, and future governance civilizations.

## Scope

- Constitutional lineage doctrine.
- Governance inheritance philosophy.
- Multi-generation governance continuity standards.
- Institutional succession continuity doctrine.
- Phase 10 constitutional identity preservation.
- Governance stewardship lineage architecture.
- Legitimacy inheritance verification doctrine.
- Governance continuity succession chains.
- Institutional memory inheritance standards.
- Constitutional stewardship preservation philosophy.

## Required Safeguards

- No runtime activation exists.
- No execution authorization exists.
- No deployment authorization exists.
- No provider onboarding exists.
- No orchestration activation exists.
- No infrastructure activation exists.
- No lineage or succession layer creates runtime authority.
- No inheritance process bypasses constitutional supremacy.
- No governance transfer layer enables hidden execution escalation.
- No stewardship doctrine weakens auditability.

## Step Improvement Notes

- Correct build order: follows restoration continuity and archive recovery doctrine.
- Safe to include now: lineage doctrine, succession continuity, legitimacy inheritance, stewardship lineage, memory inheritance, and multi-generation continuity.
- Future upgrade: stewardship acceptance packet and succession readiness checklist.
- Do not include: runtime enablement, providers, adapters, deployment paths, credentials, OAuth, APIs, fetch/axios, Prisma/Supabase changes, background jobs, autonomous governance, automated succession authority, governance self-transfer systems, or execution systems.
